page: Amazon Web Services r8gd.2xlarge is a memory-optimized instance powered by an AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 processor. It features 8 dedicated physical cores and 8 vCPUs running at 2.8 GHz, with no hyperthreading. The hardware configuration includes 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. Local storage is provided via a 474 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume, and baseline network bandwidth is rated at 4 Gbps.

In benchmark testing, the r8gd.2xlarge demonstrates strong memory performance, with a PassMark memory score in the top 25% and a cached small-block read memory bandwidth of 925.12 GB/sec. Large-block read and write memory bandwidths reach 169.31 GB/sec and 68.7 GB/sec, respectively. CPU performance is generally average, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1760.0 and a multi-core score of 9652.0. However, cryptographic performance is a relative weakness, with the PassMark CPU encryption test scoring in the bottom 25%.

This instance is designed for memory-intensive workloads that benefit from local NVMe storage, such as caching layers, database operations, and real-time analytics. It achieves 16.62 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks and 19.2 GB/sec in static web serving throughput. While it handles light LLM inference, performance drops significantly on larger models, with medium 7B model prompt processing falling into the bottom 10%. The ARM64 architecture and dedicated core allocation provide predictable resource density and qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound applications compared to traditional x86 alternatives.
description: Amazon Web Services r8gd.2xlarge is an ARM64 memory-optimized instance featuring 8 dedicated physical cores on an AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 processor running at 2.8 GHz. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of memory, yielding 8.0 GB per core, and includes a 474 GB NVMe SSD local instance store. The instance delivers strong memory performance, scoring in the top 25% of the PassMark memory benchmark, alongside a cached small-block read bandwidth of 925.12 GB/sec. However, cryptographic operations represent a performance tradeoff, scoring in the bottom 25% on encryption tests. With a baseline network speed of 4 Gbps, the r8gd.2xlarge is optimized for memory-intensive and storage-backed workloads such as database operations, caching layers, and web serving, while showing performance limitations in complex LLM inference tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r8gd.2xlarge is an ARM64 memory-optimized instance with 8 Graviton vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and a 474 GB NVMe SSD, delivering strong memory bandwidth for database and caching workloads.

tagline: An ARM64 memory-optimized instance featuring dedicated Graviton cores, high-bandwidth memory, and local NVMe SSD storage for database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 processor with 8 dedicated physical cores
64.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per core
474 GB local NVMe SSD instance store volume
Strong memory performance scoring in the top 25% of PassMark benchmarks
Weak cryptographic and large-model LLM prompt processing performance
Suited for database operations, Redis caching, and static web serving
